commit.message:
feat: add cli-mastery skill — interactive Copilot CLI training (#915)

* feat: add cli-mastery skill — interactive Copilot CLI training

Adds cli-mastery, an interactive training system for the GitHub Copilot CLI.
8 modules covering slash commands, keyboard shortcuts, modes, agents, skills,
MCP, configuration, and advanced techniques. Includes scenario challenges,
a final exam, XP/leveling system, and SQL-based progress tracking.

Source: https://github.com/DUBSOpenHub/copilot-cli-mastery (MIT)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address review feedback on frontmatter and consistency

- Switch description from folded block scalar (>) to single-quoted string
  per AGENTS.md documented format
- Fix Module 7 heading: backtick-wrap @ separately from 'file mentions'
  to avoid implying '@ file mentions' is a literal command
- Fix Final Exam Q6: change '@ + filename' to '@filename' with example
  to match the @src/auth.ts syntax taught in modules
- Fix Final Exam Q7: add GEMINI.md to match Module 7 precedence list

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: rename curriculum/ to references/ per agentskills.io spec

Addresses review feedback from @aaronpowell on PR #915.
The Agent Skills specification defines references/ as the standard
directory for supplementary documentation that agents read on demand.

- Renamed skills/cli-mastery/curriculum/ → references/
- Updated all path references in SKILL.md
- Updated asset paths in docs/README.skills.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: widen ReconnectState test window to fix CI failure (#321)

## CI Failure

`ReconnectState_ShouldCarryIsMultiAgentSession` fails in CI
([run](https://github.com/PureWeen/PolyPilot/actions/runs/22836953792/job/66235226971))
with:

```
Assert.Contains() Failure: Sub-string not found
String:    "newState.HasUsedToolsThisTurn = state.Has"···
Not found: "newState.IsMultiAgentSession = state.IsMu"···
```

## Root Cause

This source-scanning test checks that `IsMultiAgentSession` is carried
forward near `HasUsedToolsThisTurn` in the reconnect block of
`SendPromptAsync`. The test uses a 200-char search window, but the
`SuccessfulToolCountThisTurn` carry-forward line (added in a later PR
between the two) pushed `IsMultiAgentSession` beyond that window.

The production code is correct — `IsMultiAgentSession` IS properly
carried forward at line 2471.

## Fix

Widened the search window from 200 → 400 chars to accommodate
intermediate carry-forward lines.

All 2279 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: jeffredodd <2861516+jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor validate_config to check type once before range validation

Co-authored-by: jeffredodd <2861516+jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

* Move refresh interval support from Dad Jokes into generic PluginBase

Refactored the refresh_seconds / caching feature so it is available
to ALL plugins via the base class, not just Dad Jokes.

PluginBase changes (src/plugins/base.py):
- Added get_data() method with automatic time-based caching
- Added refresh_seconds property (reads from manifest settings_schema)
- Added clear_cache() method
- Added _validate_refresh_seconds() for manifest-driven validation
- Cache auto-clears on config change or plugin disable

PluginRegistry changes (src/plugins/registry.py):
- fetch_plugin_data() now calls plugin.get_data() (cached) instead
  of plugin.fetch_data() (always fresh)
- set_plugin_config() runs base refresh_seconds validation alongside
  plugin-specific validate_config()

Plugin cleanups:
- dad_jokes: removed plugin-specific caching, validation, and constants;
  fetch_data() is now a pure API call, caching handled by base
- spacecraft_launches: removed refresh_seconds validation (base handles it)
- sports_scores: removed refresh_seconds validation (base handles it)
- wsdot: removed refresh_seconds validation (base handles it)
- _template: removed manual refresh_seconds validation example

How it works for any plugin:
- If a plugin's manifest.json has refresh_seconds in settings_schema,
  get_data() automatically caches results for that interval
- If no refresh_seconds in manifest, get_data() delegates directly
  to fetch_data() with no caching (backwards compatible)
- Validation min/max bounds are read from the manifest schema

Tests: 31 new base class tests + updated plugin tests (259 total pass)

Co-authored-by: Jeffrey D Johnson <jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add hard rate-limit floor (min_refresh_seconds) to prevent 429s

Adds a non-user-configurable rate-limit floor that is enforced at
runtime, regardless of how plugin config is loaded or set.

Manifest changes:
- New top-level 'min_refresh_seconds' field added to all 17 plugin
  manifests that define refresh_seconds.  This is the absolute minimum
  interval a plugin will ever use, set by the plugin developer.

PluginBase changes (src/plugins/base.py):
- New 'min_refresh_seconds' property: reads the manifest field first,
  falls back to settings_schema minimum, then global MIN_REFRESH_SECONDS
- 'refresh_seconds' property now clamps to the floor at runtime -- even
  if stored config somehow contains a lower value, the plugin will never
  poll faster than min_refresh_seconds
- '_validate_refresh_seconds' uses the same floor for config validation

CI validation (tests/test_plugin_validation.py):
- min_refresh_seconds must be a positive integer
- min_refresh_seconds must be <= settings_schema minimum (consistency)
- Plugins with refresh_seconds must declare min_refresh_seconds

Tests: 13 new tests covering the floor property, runtime clamping,
       fallback chain, and manifest consistency (all pass)

Co-authored-by: Jeffrey D Johnson <jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix CI: relax rate-limit floor test to accept schema minimum as fallback

The test_plugins_with_refresh_seconds_have_floor test strictly required
an explicit min_refresh_seconds field in every plugin manifest. This
broke when the generic_data plugin (added to main after our branch
forked) has refresh_seconds with a schema minimum but no explicit
min_refresh_seconds.

PluginBase already falls back to the schema minimum at runtime, so the
test now accepts either an explicit min_refresh_seconds OR a minimum in
the settings_schema refresh_seconds property.

Co-authored-by: jeffredodd <2861516+jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: US-003 - Ensure health fields are excluded from public profile endpoints

Add stripHealthFields() helper that removes heightCm, weightKg, dateOfBirth,
workoutGoal, unitPreference, and healthProfilePromptDismissed from user data.
Applied to GET /users/:uid, GET /users/:uid/profile, and GET /users/search
endpoints as defense-in-depth. GET /users/me still returns all health fields.

* chore: update PRD and progress for US-004

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: US-005 - Add Health & Body section with unit preference to account settings

Add Health & Body section to the account settings page with:
- Unit preference toggle (metric/imperial) with auto-detection from navigator.language
- Height input: cm for metric, feet+inches for imperial with conversion
- Weight input: kg for metric, lbs for imperial with conversion
- Date of birth input with computed age display
- Privacy indicator (lock icon + 'Private — only visible to you')
- All fields save on blur via PUT /users/me mutation
- Follows existing React Query mutation pattern

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: US-006 - Add Workout Goal selector to account settings

Add Workout Goal section below Health & Body with:
- 5 goal options as selectable cards in responsive grid (2 cols, 3 on sm+)
- Uses WORKOUT_GOAL_LABELS from @burnbuddy/shared for emoji and label text
- Selected goal highlighted with primary color border/background/ring
- Click to select, click again to deselect (sends null to clear)
- Shows current goal status text or 'No goal selected'
- Saves immediately via healthFieldMutation (PUT /users/me)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: US-007 - Add onboarding health profile prompt for new users

Add HealthProfilePrompt component shown on dashboard when
healthProfilePromptDismissed is falsy. Contains:
- Unit preference toggle (auto-detected from navigator.language)
- Height input (metric/imperial)
- Weight input (metric/imperial)
- Date of birth input
- Workout goal selector cards
- Skip button (dismisses prompt only)
- Save & Continue button (saves all fields + dismisses)

Also adds 'Complete your health profile' banner to account settings
when prompt was dismissed but no health fields are set.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
🔄 Reboot: Pivot from Scoring Dashboard to Accountability Platform (#10)

* Initial plan

* feat: reboot to face-first politician accountability platform

- Delete old policy scoring dashboard (upstream ETL, scoring engine, demo)
- Add migration 0005: photos pipeline, actions, donations, promises, foreign_ties tables
- Rebuild src/lib/api.ts: response helpers, KV cache, admin/internal auth
- Rebuild src/lib/db.ts: queries for all new accountability tables
- Add src/lib/photos.ts: R2 photo pipeline and APH photo fetch helpers
- Add /api/v1/politicians.json and /api/v1/politicians/[id].json endpoints
- Add /api/internal/etl/roster: APH member sync with party upsert
- Add /api/internal/photos/fetch and /serve: photo pipeline with R2 storage
- Add /api/admin/politicians/[id]/{actions,donations,promises,foreign-ties}
- New homepage: dark grid UI with chamber/party/search filters
- New /politicians/[id] profile page: tabbed actions/money/promises/foreign ties
- New /admin UI: ETL controls and record add form with Basic Auth
- Update wrangler.json: add Workers AI binding, update cron schedules
- Update worker-wrapper.js: map crons to new roster/photo endpoints
- Update src/env.d.ts: add AI, SESSION, ADMIN_PASSWORD, INTERNAL_SECRET
- Update .env.example with new variables

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address code review - timing-safe auth, name filter, photo DB error logging

- Use constant-time string comparison in requireAdminAuth (api.ts) to prevent
  timing attacks during Basic Auth password validation
- Apply same timing-safe comparison in admin/index.astro server-side auth check
- Use filter(Boolean).join(' ') for politician name to avoid extra whitespace
- Add explicit error logging for DB query failure in photos/fetch ETL endpoint

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct User-Agent string spacing in roster ETL and photos lib

commit.message:
Move example PO loading into the empty state and isolate localized sample loading logic (#14)

The example PO action was previously exposed in the header and always
loaded as `hello-dolly-nl_NL.po`, even when a localized sample was
fetched. This change keeps the action scoped to the empty state and
makes localized example loading produce a filename that matches the
fetched PO language.

- **Empty-state example flow**
  - Keeps `Load example PO` exclusive to the empty-state upload surface.
- Preserves the existing click-to-upload empty state while preventing
the example action from triggering the file picker.

- **Localized example asset handling**
- Derives the example filename from the fetched PO `Language` header
when WordPress returns a localized sample.
- Falls back to the bundled Dutch sample only when no localized asset is
available.
  - Avoids inventing locale filenames from ambiguous language codes.

- **Extraction into `src/lib/example-po`**
- Moves locale detection, WordPress export URL construction, PO
validation, filename derivation, and in-memory caching into:
    - `src/lib/example-po/index.ts`
- Keeps `Index.tsx` focused on page behavior instead of
transport/parsing details.

- **Deterministic test support**
- Adds a cache reset helper for tests so example PO fetch state does not
leak across runs.
  - Adds focused unit coverage for:
    - locale detection
    - WordPress URL generation
    - localized filename derivation
    - malformed locale fallback
    - cache behavior

```ts
const preferredTargetLanguage = getDeviceExampleTargetLanguage();
const examplePo =
  (await fetchExamplePoFromWordPress(preferredTargetLanguage)) ?? getBundledExamplePo();

const result = parsePOFileWithDiagnostics(examplePo.content, examplePo.filename);
```

- **Behavioral effect**
  - A fetched German sample now loads as `hello-dolly-de_DE.po`.
- Malformed locale headers fall back cleanly instead of being silently
rewritten.

<screenshot>

![Updated empty state example PO
action](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e96f3c85-144e-4565-9458-7b6be0da9969)

</screenshot>

Fix MIPS cross-compilation build failure

Busybox was enabled by default in the Buildroot config but requires
utmpx.h, which is disabled in the uclibc config (UCLIBC_HAS_UTMPX unset).
This caused the Buildroot build to fail, preventing nlohmann_json, spdlog,
and other target packages from being installed to the sysroot. CMake then
failed with 'find_package(nlohmann_json)' not found.

- Disable busybox in mfi_defconfig (not needed: BR2_INIT_NONE and
  BR2_SYSTEM_BIN_SH_NONE are already set)
- Add RESULT_VARIABLE checks to execute_process calls in br2/CMakeLists.txt
  so Buildroot failures are caught immediately instead of silently ignored

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Add tests for Series.scanValues, scanAllValues, foldValues, and reduceValues (#599)

🤖 *This PR was created by Repo Assist, an automated AI assistant.*

## Summary

Adds 7 new tests covering four public `Series` functions that previously
had zero test coverage: `scanValues`, `scanAllValues`, `foldValues`, and
`reduceValues`.

### Tests added

| Test | What it verifies |
|---|---|
| `Series.scanValues accumulates running sum correctly` | Basic
accumulation over a complete series |
| `Series.scanValues skips missing values and leaves them missing` |
Missing entries remain missing in the output; accumulator carries
forward from the last present value |
| `Series.foldValues computes running product correctly` | Basic
aggregate over a complete series |
| `Series.foldValues skips missing values` | Missing entries are
silently skipped |
| `Series.reduceValues computes sum of series` | Basic reduce over a
complete series |
| `Series.reduceValues skips missing values` | Missing entries are
silently skipped |
| `Series.scanAllValues sees missing values and can carry forward` |
`scanAllValues` exposes missing entries as `None` to the user function,
allowing custom carry-forward logic |
